disclaimer.p1: NEXBRIDGE® is a registered trademark owned by NexBridge Digital Financial Solutions S.A. de C.V., a company organized and existing under the laws of the Republic of El Salvador. All products and services are subject to the applicable digital asset legislation of the Republic of El Salvador and are offered under regulatory public oversight. Any reference to products or services shall be understood as referring solely to those products and services that have been duly authorized for offer or sale by the competent authorities of the Republic of El Salvador. The digital assets described on this website are issued from the Republic of El Salvador and any reference to an 'offer', 'issuance' or 'availability' shall be understood as an offer made from El Salvador and governed exclusively by Salvadoran law and regulatory authorization. Access to such digital assets from other jurisdictions is subject to applicable local laws, eligibility requirements and the relevant Terms and Conditions, and may be restricted or unavailable. Nothing on this website constitutes an offer or solicitation in any jurisdiction where such offer or solicitation would be unlawful.
disclaimer.p2: Where digital assets are made accessible through authorized distributors or licensed intermediaries, such access is provided under the regulatory responsibility of such distributors or intermediaries, in accordance with applicable local laws and licensing requirements. Nothing contained in this website is intended as investment advice on digital assets. Every investor must read and fully understand the relevant issuance prospectuses, termsheets and other documentation published in connection with the issuance, offer or sale of digital assets. Such documentation contains all applicable risk factors relating to the digital assets being offered.
disclaimer.copyright: Copyright © 2025–2026 for NEXBRIDGE®. All rights reserved.

CookieConsent.title: We use cookies
CookieConsent.body: By accepting all cookies, you consent to the use of this technology to enhance your experience and for analysis and marketing purposes. You can visit our <settingsLink>Cookie Settings</settingsLink> and our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink> for more information.
CookieConsent.settingsDesc: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit our website. They allow the website to recognize your device and collect information about your interactions. You can choose not to allow some cookies, but blocking them may affect your experience on the site.

meta.description: Learn about NexBridge, a licensed digital asset issuer bringing institutional-grade products to eligible markets under El Salvador's Digital Assets Issuance Law.

hero.label: About NexBridge
hero.title:
Building the Future of

Bitcoin-Powered Finance
hero.subtitle: A regulated issuer building the foundation of Bitcoin-powered finance — connecting traditional capital markets with blockchain efficiency since 2023.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.desc: To set a new standard for transparent, regulated, and independently audited digital issuances — built with the rigor of traditional markets and the efficiency of Bitcoin infrastructure.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.desc: We believe innovation and regulatory certainty must work together — not in conflict — to build trust and unlock new possibilities for capital markets. Institutional-grade digital instruments, accessible across authorized markets, under clear public regulation.

founded.title: Founded — 2023
founded.desc: NexBridge was founded in El Salvador to build regulated digital issuance infrastructure on Bitcoin rails, connecting traditional capital markets with blockchain efficiency.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.desc: Expanding access to high-quality digital assets by bringing investment opportunities on-chain with robust regulatory standards and familiar traditional finance dynamics through licensed venues and regulated intermediaries.

ustblLaunch.title: USTBL Launch — Milestone
ustblLaunch.desc: Launched USTBL, our first regulated digital instrument linked to U.S. Treasury Bills on Bitcoin infrastructure, proving real-world asset exposure can be delivered on-chain with public-market rigor. Reached $30M soft cap within 48 hours of launch (November 2024).

whatsNext.title: What's Next
whatsNext.desc: Broadening our product suite, growing distribution through additional regulated exchanges, preparing licensed OTC Desk launch, and scaling distribution via authorized distributors and market participants.

licenses.subtitle: Operating under clear public regulation across multiple jurisdictions.

meta.title: FAQs — NexBridge Digital Assets
meta.description: Frequently asked questions about NexBridge digital assets, USTBL, regulatory compliance, trading venues, and how to get started.

hero.label: FAQ
hero.title: Frequently Asked <gradient>Questions</gradient>
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about NexBridge, our products, and how to get started.

whatIsNexbridge.a: NEXBRIDGE is a regulated issuer of digital assets structured with an ETF-like design, based on published rules, and issued natively on Bitcoin's Liquid Network. Each token includes a public term sheet defining fees, rules, risks, and investor rights with dedicated registration numbers.

compareToEtfs.q: How do NEXBRIDGE tokens compare to ETFs?
compareToEtfs.a: Like traditional ETFs, NEXBRIDGE tokens track reference markets under clear, published rules. They offer transparency through documented rescaling mechanisms, disclosed fees, fair NAV-based subscriptions/redemptions, regulatory clarity at the token level, proof of reserves, institutional settlement rails, and liquidity focus through OTC and selective exchange listings.

howRegulated.a: Duly authorized by El Salvador's National Commission of Digital Assets (CNAD), under the registration number EAD-0005. Each issuance receives unique registration numbers. Permissions include issuer licensing, digital asset exchange, platform placement, and investment product management.

whyLiquid.q: Why build on Liquid Network (Bitcoin technology)?
whyLiquid.a: Liquid Network provides Bitcoin-anchored assurance, policy-based controls for regulated issuance, predictable efficient settlement, mature security models, and confidential-transaction capabilities reducing front-running risks while preserving verifiability.

proofOfReserve.q: How do you execute Proof of Reserve (PoR)?
proofOfReserve.a: Currently monthly PoR attestations by Grant Thornton verify reserves and reconciliations. Phase 2 adds hybrid PoR combining monthly attestations with automatic on-chain verification of custody accounts and blockchain addresses.

whoSafeguards.a: Independent, regulated custodians hold segregated, issuance-specific reserves. Controls include dual-control on NAV and mint/burn operations, documented time-stamped approvals, daily reconciliations, and monthly independent assurance attestations.

multichain.a: The canonical issuance exists on Bitcoin technology as the single source of truth. Future access wrappers on other chains would mirror the same RID rules, maintain 1:1 supply ratios, and settle back to the canonical chain.

whatIsUstbl.a: USTBL provides short-term fixed-income digital asset exposure (Treasury-style) issued on Bitcoin infrastructure, delivering transparent integrated yield and serving as a benchmark, liquidity anchor, and structured product foundation.

whatProducts.a: Currently offering USTBL (short-term fixed-income exposure). Coming soon: tokenized equities, innovative fixed-income products, and native on-chain equity issuance. Also available: bespoke digital assets for institutions.

ustblMoreThanCollateral.q: Why is USTBL more than "just collateral"?
ustblMoreThanCollateral.a: USTBL functions as an on-chain fixed-income benchmark, gateway for institutional de-risk strategies, liquidity anchor for market-making/lending, structured notes foundation, and pays clear integrated yield to holders.

whoCanAccess.q: Who can access NEXBRIDGE assets today?
whoCanAccess.a: Primary market access targets professional/institutional investors (funds, banks, brokers, family offices) requiring KYC/AML and address whitelisting. Secondary market access for self-directed users in eligible jurisdictions depends on exchange-specific rules and jurisdiction restrictions.

otcOnboarding.q: How does the OTC Desk onboarding process work?
otcOnboarding.a: Complete KYC/AML verification per the issuance's RID, register settlement addresses for whitelisting, sign OTC terms, and receive desk contacts and cut-off times before subscription/redemption capabilities activate.

subscriptionsRedemptions.q: How do subscriptions and redemptions work?
subscriptionsRedemptions.a: Phase 1 (current): Pre-fund orders, execute at end-of-day NAV with T+1 token activation. Redemptions lock tokens at T and process payouts T+1 at NAV. Phase 2 (upcoming): RFQ for larger requests and optional collateralized margin wallets supporting faster settlement.

whereTokensHeld.a: Tokens remain in either self-custody through approved whitelisted addresses (like Blockstream Green/Jade) or exchange custody at regulated CEXs listing the issuance. Transfers only occur between approved addresses.

feesAndCosts.q: Where can I find all fees and costs?
feesAndCosts.a: OTC fees appear in OTC Desk T&Cs and each issuance's RID; exchange fees vary by venue with spreads depending on order-book liquidity. No hidden costs; all rules and fees are published in the RID and venue fee schedules.

nearTermFocus.a: Running the OTC Desk with expanding features (best execution, RFQ, margin funding), expanding authorized self-custody wallets, rolling out selective CEX listings with professional market makers, and publishing updates on regulated stablecoin integration.

longTermVision.a: Building a Bitcoin-first, interoperable capital-markets stack where institutions can access, structure, and settle real-world exposures on-chain with ETF-like clarity — starting from benchmarks, expanding to innovative structures, and graduating to native equity issuance.

help.title: Still have questions?
help.desc: Visit our Help Center for step-by-step guides, wallet tutorials, and onboarding support.

meta.title: Glossary — NexBridge Digital Asset Terms
meta.description: Key terms and definitions for understanding digital assets, regulation, and the NexBridge ecosystem.

hero.title: NexBridge <gradient>Glossary</gradient>
hero.subtitle: Key terms and definitions for understanding digital assets, regulation, and the NexBridge ecosystem.

aml.definition: Laws and procedures designed to prevent the use of financial systems for money laundering or terrorist financing.

bestExecution.definition: A framework ensuring client trades are executed at the most favorable terms available, minimizing slippage and protecting investors.

collateral.definition: Assets pledged to secure a financial position, often used in lending or settlement.

cnad.definition: The regulatory authority in El Salvador supervising digital asset issuances and compliance.

custodian.definition: A regulated institution safeguarding the underlying real-world assets.

dasp.definition: Licensed entities authorized to operate exchanges, custody, or distribution of regulated digital assets.

kyc.definition: Regulatory process of verifying client identity before allowing financial transactions.

liquidNetwork.definition: A Bitcoin Layer-2 sidechain developed by Blockstream, designed for fast, confidential, and secure settlement of digital assets.

nav.definition: The per-token value of underlying assets minus liabilities and fees, calculated daily.

otcDesk.definition: A regulated primary market service enabling direct subscriptions and redemptions outside public order books.

por.definition: Cryptographic or audited evidence showing that an issuer holds the assets backing its tokens.

redemption.definition: The act of exchanging tokens back for the underlying asset or cash equivalent.

rid.definition: A regulated disclosure document required under El Salvador's Digital Asset Issuance Law, equivalent to a term sheet or prospectus.

selfCustody.definition: Holding digital assets directly in one's own wallet, rather than with a custodian.

serialIssuer.definition: A regulated entity authorized to launch multiple digital asset products under the same legal framework.

tPlusOne.definition: Settlement cycle where trades are finalized one business day after the transaction date.

tokenization.definition: The process of converting rights to an asset into a digital token on blockchain.

transfer.definition: The process of moving digital assets from one wallet to another within the blockchain network.

wysiwyg.term: "What you see is what you get" pricing
wysiwyg.definition: Transparent execution where fees and pricing are disclosed upfront with no hidden costs.

whitelistedWallet.definition: A digital wallet address approved through KYC/AML checks, authorized to receive and redeem regulated tokens.

meta.title: Legal Framework — NexBridge Regulatory Compliance
meta.description: NexBridge's legal framework: licensed under El Salvador's Digital Assets Issuance Law, regulated by the National Digital Assets Commission (CNAD).

hero.label: Legal
hero.title: Legal <gradient>Framework</gradient>
hero.subtitle: NEXBRIDGE operates under El Salvador's Digital Assets Issuance Law, maintaining robust governance, strict AML/CFT policies, and independent third-party audits for regulated digital asset issuance.

regulatoryProcess.title: Regulatory Process
regulatoryProcess.subtitle: Every NEXBRIDGE product follows El Salvador's Digital Assets Issuance Law through four steps.

certified.title: Certified
certified.desc: Every product is validated by an independent approved certifier

registered.title: Registered
registered.desc: Official registration and approval by CNAD, El Salvador's National Commission of Digital Assets

audited.title: Audited
audited.desc: Reserves and performance are verified monthly by Grant Thornton; NAV is updated daily

supervised.title: Supervised
supervised.desc: Ongoing oversight ensures operational integrity and regulatory clarity with all applicable laws

authorizations.title: Regulatory Authorizations
authorizations.subtitle: NEXBRIDGE operates under these regulatory authorizations.

regulatoryBodies.title: Regulatory Bodies
regulatoryBodies.subtitle: The institutions that oversee and authorize NEXBRIDGE operations.

cnad.title: CNAD: National Commission for Digital Assets
cnad.desc: El Salvador's official regulatory body responsible for granting licenses, approving issuances and certifiers, registering digital asset service providers, supervising operations, and ensuring compliance with the Digital Assets Issuance Law.

cnv.title: CNV: Argentina's Securities Commission
cnv.desc: NEXBRIDGE is registered as Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP No. 117) under Argentina's Comisión Nacional de Valores (CNV), reinforcing regional presence as Argentina's digital asset regulations develop.

lei.title: Legal Entity Identifier (LEI)
lei.desc: NEXBRIDGE holds a global LEI supervised by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF). This international identifier proves legal identity to regulators, partners, and investors while supporting vLEI adoption for additional fraud protection.

compliance.title: Compliance Features
compliance.subtitle: Built-in safeguards that ensure every issuance meets institutional standards.

kycAml.title: KYC / AML
kycAml.desc: Full identity verification and anti-money laundering checks aligned with FATF/GAFI standards for every participant.

proofOfReserves.title: Proof of Reserves
proofOfReserves.desc: Monthly independent verification by Grant Thornton confirms that all issued tokens are fully backed by underlying assets.

investorProtection.title: Investor Protection
investorProtection.desc: Assets are held in segregated custody with regulated counterparties, ensuring complete separation from operational funds.

dailyNav.title: Daily NAV Updates
dailyNav.desc: Net Asset Value is calculated and published daily, providing real-time transparency on the value of each token.

transparentReporting.title: Transparent Reporting
transparentReporting.desc: All key documents, including prospectuses and audit reports, are publicly available through CNAD's official registry.

serialIssuer.title: Serial Issuer License
serialIssuer.desc: NEXBRIDGE holds a Serial Issuer license enabling streamlined issuance of multiple regulated digital assets under one authorization.

lookingAhead.title: Looking Ahead
lookingAhead.desc: As regulatory frameworks for digital assets evolve worldwide, NEXBRIDGE monitors changes and expands licensing step-by-step — ensuring our products remain compliant and accessible in key jurisdictions, always under clear public supervision.

meta.title: Tutorials — How to Trade NexBridge Digital Assets
meta.description: Step-by-step guides for trading NexBridge digital assets. Learn how to buy USTBL, set up a wallet, and access eligible trading venues.

hero.label: Learning Center
hero.title: Learn NEXBRIDGE — <gradient>Fast & Hands-on</gradient>
hero.subtitle: Practical tutorials to get you live on the NEXBRIDGE assets ecosystem: from wallet verification to sending regulated assets with confidence. Built for self-directed users, ops teams, and institutions.

whitelistWallet.title: How to Whitelist Your Liquid Wallet on the NEXBRIDGE OTC
whitelistWallet.desc: Create a Liquid wallet in Green, secure your seed, generate your AMP account, and add your AMP ID on the OTC Desk. Leave fully enabled to receive/transfer regulated assets from verified addresses.

sendIssuances.title: How to Send NEXBRIDGE Issuances on Liquid Network
sendIssuances.desc: Understand L-BTC vs. regulated tokens (e.g., USTBL), how whitelisting affects transfers, and what failed vs. successful transactions look like in practice. Finish with a checklist to confirm deposits and block confirmations.

importWallet.title: How to Import Your Wallet to Blockstream App
importWallet.desc: Restore or import your existing Liquid wallet in minutes. Follow best practices for seed handling and verification to ensure a clean, secure setup. For this example we're using Blockstream App (Green Wallet).

cta.title: Need More Help?
cta.desc: Visit our Help Center for step-by-step guides, troubleshooting, and onboarding support.
